title: Untitled document.pdf
summaries:
* The Himalayas is a mountain range in Asia separating the plains of the Indian subcontinent from the Tibetan Plateau.
* The range contains over 100 peaks exceeding 7,200 metres (23,600 feet) above sea level, including Mount Everest.
* It spans five countries: Nepal, India, China, Bhutan, and Pakistan, with disputed sovereignty in the Kashmir region.
* The range is bordered by the Karakoram and Hindu Kush ranges on the northwest, the Tibetan Plateau on the north, and the Indo-Gangetic Plain on the south.
* Major rivers rising in the vicinity of the Himalayas include the Indus, the Ganges, and the Tsangpo–Brahmaputra.
* There are 53 million people living in the Himalayas, and 600 million people living in the combined drainage basin of its rivers.

* The combined drainage basin of the Himalayas is home to some 600 million people, and 53 million people live in the mountains.
* The range has profoundly shaped the cultures of South Asia and Tibet, and many peaks are sacred in Hinduism and Buddhism.
* Climbers are restricted from the summits of Kangchenjunga (from the Indian side), Gangkhar Puensum, Machapuchare, Nanda Devi, and Kailash in the Tibetan Transhimalaya.
* The range was uplifted by the collision of the Indian tectonic plate with the Eurasian plate, specifically by the folding or nappe-formation of the uppermost Indian crust.
* The mountain range runs west-northwest to east-southeast in an arc 2,400 km (1,500 mi) long.

* The mantle subducted under Eurasia.
* The Himalayan mountain range runs west-northwest to east-southeast in an arc 2,400 km (1,500 mi) long.
* The range's western anchor is Nanga Parbat (south of the Indus river's northernmost bend), and its eastern anchor is Namcha Barwa (west of the Yarlung Tsangpo River's great bend).
* The Indus-Yarlung suture zone separates the Himalayas from the Tibetan plateau.
* The Indus and Yarlung Tsangpo rivers separate the Himalayas from the Karakorams, the Hindu Kush, and the Transhimalaya.
* The range varies in width from 350 kilometres (220 miles) in the west to 151 km (94 mi) in the east.
